marketState: neutral, tilted bullish near-term on an extreme heat-driven demand spike, capped medium-term by storage surplus and weak seasonality
summary: Front price ($2.948) is sitting almost exactly at model fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.031, FAIR), so there is no fair-value edge right now, but the fair-value curve rises steadily to $3.067 by +21D. The dominant near-term driver is weather: cooling demand is 15.5% above the 5y norm for the next week and 23.9% above for days 8-16, both near the top of their historical range (rangePos 0.91-0.93) and still climbing (+24.7 in 24h). That argues for upside pressure into the next 1-2 weeks, reinforced by daily/4h technicals that are oversold (RSI 26-36, price below the lower Bollinger band) even as intraday momentum (5m/15m RSI 59-66, MACD positive) is already turning up. Working against this: seasonality shows only 31-38% odds of a higher price over 1-4 weeks with a -3.1% typical 4-week move (though reliability is explicitly Low), storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average after a build that beat forecast by 72 Bcf (bearish surprise), and managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile) - none of these are extreme, but together they cap conviction on the longer-term view. Net: a tactical, weather-driven bounce is favored short-term, while the 3-12 week outlook is genuinely mixed and better served by waiting for a cleaner setup.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (priority #1) is unreliable and mildly bearish (31% odds higher in 4 weeks, typical move -3.1%, 50pp reliability spread), while storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish 72 Bcf build surprise and COT shows managed money moderately net short. These offset the bullish weather trend, leaving no clean edge for a multi-week position.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a close above ~$3.10 (near the +14D/+21D fair-value path) alongside a storage deficit surprise, confirming a bullish breakout toward the $3.35-3.45 fair-value band top, or (b) a pullback into the $2.60-2.70 zone (near fair-value band lows) with weather demand cooling back toward the 5y norm, confirming a mean-reversion short toward the seasonal -3% move.

shortTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.95 (current front / minor dip toward next-month price)
shortTerm.rationale: Cooling demand is running 15-24% above the 5-year norm and still rising into days 8-16, price is fairly valued with a rising fair-value path (now $2.917 to +21D $3.067), and daily/4h are oversold (RSI 26-36, price below the lower Bollinger band) just as intraday momentum (5m/15m RSI 59-66, MACD positive, price above the upper Bollinger on 5m) is turning up - a classic short-term bounce setup into peak heat demand.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back below ~$2.78, or a sharp reversal in the weather forecast that pulls demand_1_7D/8_16D pctVs5y back toward zero, would invalidate the bounce thesis.

Price essentially at fair value now ($2.948 vs $2.917 fair) with fair value rising to $3.067 by +21D
